history.content:
<thought>
I see a vertical wall (#) column just ahead and slightly left. The cells immediately in front of me are empty floor cells (.). To the right of that wall column, there is lava (L) blocking passage on multiple rows. Since I cannot move forward into the wall, I should turn left to explore an alternate path, avoiding lava and walls.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing forward with a wall column directly in front of me and lava cells to my immediate right and right side forward diagonally. The only safe cell ahead is blocked by the wall so I cannot move forward. I need to turn left or right to find a safe path. However, turning right will face me towards lava, so the best choice is to turn left to avoid lava and walls.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The wall is now to my right, and the path straight ahead appears to be empty floor cells. There is a gap in the wall ahead and to the left. Moving forward seems safe. I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation is the same as before, indicating I haven't moved. The space ahead seems to be a floor cell, so I will try to move forward again to proceed toward the opening in the wall.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The observation has not changed after attempting to move forward twice. This suggests there is a wall directly in front of me, preventing movement forward. I should consider turning instead. Turning left would face me along the wall, while turning right might allow me to find an opening. I will turn left to check the path to my left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

CURRENT OBSERVATION:
....#??
LLL.#??
L.L.#??
L.L.#??
L.L.#??
L.L.#??
L.L^#??

<thought>
Now facing left, I see a column of lava cells (L) ahead and floor cells on my right next to the wall (#). Moving forward would lead me into lava, which is deadly. Since the cells to my immediate right are safe, it would be better to turn right to face them and move that way.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The observation looks similar to the previous ones when facing forward, showing no clear path ahead except walls. This implies I might be rotating without advancing. To avoid getting stuck, I will turn right to face down, checking for any passage to move safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing downwards at the bottom center of the view. Ahead, there is a wall (#) and to my right are deadly lava cells (L). Moving forward is blocked by the wall and moving right is not possible in a single step since I can only go forward. The path forward is blocked, so I will turn left to explore other directions.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing left now, but the path ahead still shows walls (#), meaning I cannot move forward. I will turn left again to face upward and seek an opening to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

CURRENT OBSERVATION:
....#??
LLL.#??
L.L.#??
L.L.#??
L.L.#??
L.L.#??
L.L^#??

<thought>
I am facing up. The path immediately ahead of me is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. To find a safe path, I should turn right to possibly find an opening.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
After turning right, I am now facing right. However, the path ahead is blocked by a wall (#). I need to explore other directions, so I should turn right again to face down and check if there is a path there.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Now facing down, I see a wall ahead and lava cells to the right. Moving forward is blocked, and right is unsafe due to lava. I should turn right again to face left to check if there is a safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
Facing left, I see a clear path in front of me (floor cells) and no immediate lava or walls. Moving forward appears to be safe, so I will move forward to explore this direction.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
